nodes.title: Project Scaffolding
nodes.description: Bootstrap the full tech stack: Next.js App Router with TypeScript and Tailwind, Convex backend, Clerk and Resend SDKs installed, environment variables documented. No features — just a runnable foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dev starts without errors
Convex dev server connects
Clerk, Resend, Convex packages installed
.env.local.example documents all required vars
TypeScript and ESLint compile clean

nodes.title: Database Schema
nodes.description: Define all Convex schema tables: users, designs, orders, jerseyRuns, jerseyRunResponses, and intakes. Includes field types, optional fields, and indexes for common query patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 tables defined in convex/schema.ts
Indexes defined for captainId and jerseyRunId
npx convex dev starts without schema errors
Basic queries for each table return expected shape

nodes.title: Authentication Flows
nodes.description: Wire up Clerk authentication: self-registration, invite-link registration (pre-fills order form from intake), Convex user sync via webhook, and admin flag in Clerk privateMetadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Self-registration creates user in Convex users table
Invite link pre-fills order form from intake record
/portal and /admin routes protected by Clerk middleware
Admin flag only readable server-side

nodes.title: Application Shell
nodes.description: Three Next.js App Router layouts: public marketing (nav + footer), authenticated portal (sidebar + user menu), and admin (admin sidebar). Fully responsive, mobile-first, Sidestep brand colors applied.
acceptanceCriteria: Three distinct layouts with correct route groups
All layouts responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px
Nav active states highlight current route
Sidestep teal and logo applied
Mobile hamburger menu works on portal and admin

nodes.title: Marketing Pages
nodes.description: Hero, customization options, design-to-delivery process, and FAQ sections. Static content replacing the existing brochure site. Placeholder for jersey photos until client provides assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section with CTA linking to intake form
Customization section shows fabric, neckline, sleeve options
Process section shows 3-step flow
FAQ covers minimum order, timeline, design help, Vancouver-only
Responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px

nodes.title: Pricing Calculator
nodes.description: Interactive public calculator: quantity input, design fee toggle, instant estimate. Pure client-side. Tiers: 1-9=0, 10-25=0, 26-50=5, 51+=0 per jersey. 50 optional design fee. Unit tested at all tier boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 pricing tiers calculate correctly
Design fee toggle adds 50
Output labeled as estimate
Edge cases at tier boundaries pass unit tests
CTA below calculator links to intake form

nodes.title: Public Intake Form
nodes.description: Lead-capture form on the marketing site: name, team, sport, quantity, brief. Saves to Convex intakes table. No account required. Intake ID becomes the invite token for portal registration.
acceptanceCriteria: Form saves to Convex intakes table on submit
All required fields validated before submission
Success confirmation shown after submit
Intake record includes submittedAt timestamp
Mobile responsive

nodes.title: Customer Portal Dashboard
nodes.description: Authenticated portal home: captain's orders list with status chips, designs list, and empty states. Real-time via Convex subscription. Hub for all customer activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows My Orders with customer-facing status chips
Shows My Designs with file count
Empty states with CTAs for new users
Real-time updates without page refresh
Unauthenticated users redirected to sign-in

nodes.title: Design Creation
nodes.description: Design creation form in the portal: title, brief, optional Canva link, multi-file upload (any file type) to Convex storage. Design detail page with download links. Edit mode to add more files.
acceptanceCriteria: Form creates design record in Convex with uploaded file IDs
Files stored in Convex storage and retrievable via URL
Design appears in portal dashboard after save
Design detail shows all files as download links
Upload progress indicator shown

nodes.title: Order Creation
nodes.description: Full order creation form: team name, sport, quantity, jersey style, neckline, sleeve style, own-design checkbox, and design-linking multi-select. Creates order with initial Inquiry internal stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Order created in Convex with all required fields
Initial internalStages set to Inquiry
Captain can link existing designs to the order
Order appears in dashboard with Order Started status chip
Redirects to order detail page after creation

nodes.title: Order Status Tracking
nodes.description: Order detail page in the portal: 8-stage customer-facing timeline, linked designs, order specs. Real-time Convex subscription. Internal stage names never shown to customer.
acceptanceCriteria: 8 customer-facing stages shown as visual timeline
Current stage highlighted, past stages marked complete
Stage derives from internalStages in real-time
Internal stage names not visible to customer
Linked designs shown with download links

nodes.title: Jersey Run Creation
nodes.description: Captain creates a jersey run from the order detail page: jersey sizes, names mode (open/fixed), fixed roster, custom questions, deadline. Generates a shareable public URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Jersey run created with namesMode, sizes, customQuestions, and deadline
Fixed roster allows captain to pre-define names and numbers
Shareable link /run/<id> generated and displayed with copy button
Link is accessible without authentication
Up to 5 custom questions can be added

nodes.title: Jersey Run Public Form
nodes.description: Public fan submission form at /run/<id>: no account required. Adapts to open vs fixed names mode. Warns on blank name or number. Shows closed state when deadline passes or run is closed.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible at /run/<id> without authentication
Blank name or number triggers warning before submit
Fixed mode shows roster dropdown, open mode shows free text
Submission creates jerseyRunResponse record
Closed state shown when run is expired or closed

nodes.title: Jersey Run Captain Dashboard
nodes.description: Authenticated captain view of their jersey run responses: live table of all submissions, participation count, deadline countdown, and running price estimate. Real-time via Convex.
acceptanceCriteria: Response table shows name, email, size, jersey name/number, custom answers
Participation count displayed prominently
Deadline countdown shows days remaining
Running price estimate shown using pricing tiers
Data updates in real-time without page refresh

nodes.title: Admin Order Overview
nodes.description: Admin-only order list across all customers: sortable table with team name, captain, sport, quantity, internal stage, date. Clicking opens admin order detail with all fields and captain contact info.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only route returns 403 for non-admin users
Orders table shows all customers orders with correct columns
Sortable by any column
Admin order detail shows captain email and all order fields
Admin designs list shows all designs system-wide

nodes.title: Admin Stage Management
nodes.description: 14-stage internal checklist on admin order detail: admin checks/unchecks stages with timestamps. Completing a stage triggers real-time customer-facing stage update in the portal.
acceptanceCriteria: All 14 internal stages shown as checklist with timestamps
Checking a stage sets completedAt timestamp
Unchecking clears the timestamp
Customer portal stage updates in real-time after admin checks a stage
Non-admin users cannot call adminUpdateOrderStages mutation

nodes.title: Admin Customer Management
nodes.description: Admin customer list with New badge for recent signups, intake leads list with Send Invite Link button, and inline editing for any order, design, or customer record field.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer list shows all users with registration date and New badge within 7 days
Leads list shows all intake submissions with invite link copy button
Admin can inline-edit any order, design, or customer field
All mutations enforce admin-only access
New self-registered users appear prominently

nodes.title: Jersey Run Deadline Enforcement
nodes.description: Convex daily cron job closes expired jersey runs and sends Resend emails to captain and Sidestep admin. Reusable close logic also wired to manual admin close button in 3-02.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron runs daily and closes all runs where deadline < now
Resend email sent to captain and info@sidestep.design on close
Already-closed runs are skipped
Manual close by admin triggers same emails
Expired runs show closed state on public form

nodes.title: Admin Jersey Run Oversight
nodes.description: Admin page listing all jersey runs system-wide: team, captain, response count, deadline, status. Full response detail view per run. Manual close button reusing the cron closure logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin jersey runs list shows all runs across all customers
Clicking a run shows full response table with all fields
Close run button sets status to closed and sends emails
Closed runs show badge and disabled close button
Non-admin users cannot access

nodes.title: Admin Data Export
nodes.description: CSV export button on admin order detail. Downloads a file with order info and all jersey run responses formatted for supplier handoff. Admin-only API route.
acceptanceCriteria: Export CSV button triggers browser file download
CSV includes all jersey run response rows when run exists
CSV includes single order row when no run exists
Filename is sidestep-order-<team>-<date>.csv
Non-admin request to export API returns 403

nodes.title: Customer Participation History
nodes.description: Participated In section in the portal dashboard: shows jersey runs the customer submitted responses to (matched by email), with team name, jersey details, and submission date. Read-only. OBSOLETE — superseded by 3-08, which covers the dashboard section plus a per-run panel on /run/[id].
acceptanceCriteria: Participated In section shows on portal dashboard
Entries matched by respondentEmail to logged-in user email
Each entry shows team name, size, jersey name, number, and submission date
Empty state when no participations
Read-only in Phase 1

nodes.title: SEO Performance and Polish
nodes.description: Final pre-launch pass: Next.js metadata on all pages, OG image, Next.js Image optimization, jersey photos added, Lighthouse score >= 85 mobile, axe accessibility audit, zero TypeScript/ESLint errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page has unique title and meta description
OG image set on homepage
Lighthouse Performance >= 85 on mobile
Lighthouse Accessibility >= 90 on all public pages
TypeScript compiles clean with zero errors

nodes.title: Registration Email Notification
nodes.description: Resend email to info@sidestep.design when a new captain self-registers without an invite link. Triggered via the Clerk webhook user.created event. Email includes captain name and email address.
acceptanceCriteria: Resend email sent to info@sidestep.design on self-registration
Email includes captain name and email
Only fires for self-registration not invite-link registration
All tests pass

nodes.title: User Sync Architecture Revisit
nodes.description: Revisit the client-side query+mutation pattern for syncing Clerk users into Convex. Current approach runs a Convex query on every page load for authenticated users. Evaluate: Clerk webhook handled entirely as a Convex HTTP action, or a Convex auth trigger, to guarantee sync fires exactly once on sign-up with no client-side overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: Sync fires exactly once on user creation with no per-page-load queries
No unauthenticated mutations callable from outside the system
Solution works in local dev without ngrok or manual steps
Existing users table data preserved

nodes.title: Fix Mobile Responsive Shell
nodes.description: 1-04 shipped with the mobile hamburger and responsive sidebar broken. Tailwind v4 is not emitting any md: utility classes from SidebarShell (md:hidden, md:translate-x-0, md:ml-64, md:pt-0) even though sm: variants and base classes from the same file are generated correctly. Likely a Tailwind v4 + Next.js dev scan staleness issue or a quirk specific to the md: breakpoint. Once that is fixed, also reconsider the breakpoint choice (md vs lg) for tablet behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Hamburger renders and toggles the drawer at 375px and 768px on /portal and /admin
Sidebar fixed at >=1024px (or chosen desktop breakpoint)
All three layouts visually verified in a real browser at 375 / 768 / 1280
Active state highlight visible on current route
No regressions in existing routes

nodes.title: Optional Intake Image Uploads
nodes.description: Let intake-form visitors optionally attach photos or mood-board images (jerseys they like, team colors, inspiration). Files attach to the intake record so design conversations can start with visual context. Anonymous uploads create real abuse risk — open-write storage would let anyone fill our bucket — so the abuse-control approach needs a decision before implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload UI is fully optional — submitting without files works exactly as today
Abuse-control approach decided and documented (e.g. Cloudflare Turnstile, per-IP rate limit, signed upload URLs, or a server-side proxy) before any code lands
Per-file size cap and MIME allowlist (jpeg/png/webp/heic) enforced server-side, not just in the browser
Total per-intake file count and aggregate size capped
Uploaded files linked to the intake record so 2-13 admin view can preview them
Files visible alongside the intake in the admin lead view (2-13)
Mobile responsive — picker works on iOS Safari and Android Chrome

nodes.title: Extract Shared Convex Auth Helpers
nodes.description: Replace four copy-pasted requireCurrentUser implementations (orders.ts, designs.ts, jerseyRuns.ts) and the inlined admin gate (jerseyRuns.ts:closeRunByAdmin) with a single convex/_auth.ts exposing getCurrentUserOrNull, requireCurrentUser, requireAdmin, and requireOrderOwnership. Pure code motion, no behavior change. Roughly -80 lines net.
acceptanceCriteria: convex/_auth.ts created with four helpers
orders.ts, designs.ts, jerseyRuns.ts, and admin.ts import from _auth.ts instead of redefining
requireOrderOwnership replaces the inline 'load order, check captainId' pattern in three call sites
npm run typecheck passes
existing tests still pass

nodes.title: Add Convex Mutation Smoke Tests
nodes.description: Add a convex-test harness and write one happy-path plus one rejection test per public mutation in orders.ts, designs.ts, jerseyRuns.ts, intakes.ts, and users.ts. Captures trust-boundary behavior so the A-03..A-05 validation refactor has a real safety net (today the server has zero tests; only lib does).
acceptanceCriteria: convex-test installed and configured
every public mutation has at least one happy-path test
every public mutation has at least one rejection test (auth, validation, or ownership)
tests run via npm test alongside existing Vitest suite
tests pass

nodes.title: Consolidate Jersey Run Validation Rules
nodes.description: Split lib/jerseyRun.ts into rules.ts (constants and atomic pure checks) and form.ts (existing JerseyRunErrors-shaped adapter). Convex jerseyRuns.ts:create imports from rules.ts so SIZE_OPTIONS, MAX_CUSTOM_QUESTIONS, ROSTER_NAME_MAX_LENGTH and friends have a single source. Sets the template for A-04 and A-05.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/jerseyRun/rules.ts holds all constants and pure checks
lib/jerseyRun/form.ts wraps rules into the JerseyRunErrors record
convex/jerseyRuns.ts:create calls rules directly instead of redefining constants
all lib tests stay green
A-02 smoke tests still pass

nodes.title: Consolidate Jersey Run Response Validation Rules
nodes.description: Apply the A-03 split to lib/jerseyRunResponse.ts: extract rules.ts (RESPONDENT_NAME_MAX_LENGTH, EMAIL_MAX_LENGTH, ANSWER_MAX_LENGTH, EMAIL_PATTERN, atomic checks) and have convex/jerseyRuns.ts:submitResponse import from it. Removes the ~120 lines of duplicated branching in submitResponse.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/jerseyRunResponse/rules.ts and form.ts created
convex/jerseyRuns.ts:submitResponse imports constants and checks from rules.ts
duplicated branching in submitResponse replaced with rule calls
all existing tests pass
A-02 smoke tests still pass

nodes.title: Consolidate Order Design Intake Validation Rules
nodes.description: Apply the A-03 split to lib/order.ts, lib/design.ts, and lib/intake.ts. Convex orders.ts, designs.ts, and intakes.ts import constants and checks from the new rules modules. Single source for team-name, sport, quantity, design title, and brief caps.
acceptanceCriteria: rules.ts plus form.ts split done for order, design, and intake lib modules
convex/orders.ts, designs.ts, intakes.ts import constants from new rules modules
no duplicated validation constants remain across lib and convex
all existing tests pass
A-02 smoke tests still pass

nodes.title: Extract Admin Join Users By Id Helper
nodes.description: Extract the captainCache/ownerCache pattern repeated in convex/admin.ts:listOrders, listDesigns, and listJerseyRuns into a single joinUsersById helper in convex/_users.ts. Each admin query shrinks to ~6 lines: collect, joinUsersById, map. Land before 2-13 adds a fourth admin list view.
acceptanceCriteria: convex/_users.ts:joinUsersById helper created with QueryCtx-rows-keyOf signature
listOrders, listDesigns, and listJerseyRuns each shrink to ~6 lines using the helper
Unknown/empty-email fallback strings live at the call-site mapper
no behavior change for admin endpoints
all existing tests pass

nodes.title: Extract Form Hooks From Large Components
nodes.description: Optional and deferred. Extract use*Form hooks (state, validation glue, payload building, submission status) out of JerseyRunSetup (686 lines), IntakeForm (678), JerseyRunPublicForm (594), DesignForm (589), and OrderForm (544). Only worth doing if the files still feel unwieldy after A-03..A-05 land - re-measure first.
acceptanceCriteria: after A-03..A-05 land, re-measure line counts for each form component and confirm extraction is still worth doing
if confirmed, useJerseyRunForm, useIntakeForm, useJerseyRunPublicForm, useDesignForm, useOrderForm hooks live in sibling files
each form component shrinks to ~300 lines of JSX plus onSubmit wiring
all existing tests pass

nodes.title: shadcn Compatibility Smoke Test
nodes.description: Verify shadcn/ui installs cleanly against Next 16 + React 19 + Tailwind v4. A short spike runs npx shadcn init, adds Button, renders on a throwaway /dev/smoke route, and confirms the build is still green. Resolves the highest-risk open question before the migration commits.
acceptanceCriteria: npx shadcn init completes without manual workarounds
Button primitive renders on /dev/smoke
build typecheck lint test all pass
spike commit is cleanly revertible
proceed-or-stop decision recorded

nodes.title: Install shadcn Primitives and Dependencies
nodes.description: Single commit that runs npx shadcn add for the full primitive set and installs lucide-react, next-themes, sonner, react-hook-form, zod, and @hookform/resolvers. No application code references these yet — this populates the menu so the AI sees the full primitive vocabulary before refactoring begins.
acceptanceCriteria: components/ui populated with full primitive set
all runtime deps installed
components.json present
no imports yet in composites
build typecheck lint test all pass

nodes.title: Theme Provider, Dark Mode, and Theme Toggle Component
nodes.description: Wire next-themes ThemeProvider into the root layout, define shadcn dark-mode CSS variables in app/globals.css using Tailwind v4 @theme directive, and build a reusable ThemeToggle component. Toggle is not mounted in any header yet — each surface sweep will drop it in.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeProvider wraps app with attribute=class
light and dark CSS vars defined via @theme in globals.css
ThemeToggle component exists and uses useTheme
preference persists across reloads
no FOUC on initial render

nodes.title: /dev/components Showcase Page
nodes.description: Single page at /dev/components renders every shadcn primitive with usage examples. Serves as the AI agent and developer reference catalog: one URL, one file, all primitives. Includes a canonical RHF + zod + shadcn Form example that S-06 mirrors.
acceptanceCriteria: page renders every primitive at least once
labeled sections with default + 1-2 variants
includes a full Form example
includes Toaster from sonner
not linked from public navigation
renders correctly in light and dark

nodes.title: Portal Pilot — PortalShell and OrderTimeline on shadcn
nodes.description: First refactor surface. Replace inline UI in PortalShell and OrderTimeline with shadcn primitives. Mount the ThemeToggle in the portal header. Composite APIs preserved; only internal JSX changes. Improve while you are in there.
acceptanceCriteria: PortalShell uses shadcn Button Sheet Separator
OrderTimeline uses Card Badge Separator
ThemeToggle visible in portal header
exported APIs unchanged
both light and dark render correctly
build typecheck lint test pass

nodes.title: JerseyRunSetup → RHF + zod + shadcn Form (Pilot Form)
nodes.description: First form conversion. Rewrites JerseyRunSetup to use react-hook-form for state, zod for validation, and shadcn Form wrapper for fields. Proves the integration pattern with the existing Convex mutation. Becomes the template for every other form (S-07, S-08, S-10, S-11).
acceptanceCriteria: uses useForm with zodResolver
zod schema colocated
fields wrapped in shadcn Form primitives
custom questions use useFieldArray
Convex mutation receives same payload shape
validation errors via FormMessage
success via sonner toast

nodes.title: DesignForm → RHF + zod + shadcn Form
nodes.description: Apply the S-06 form pattern to DesignForm (title, brief, optional Canva link, multi-file upload). File-upload field stays imperative; RHF owns only metadata. Existing Convex storage upload flow preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: uses RHF + zodResolver + shadcn Form
zod schema colocated
file upload UX preserved
Convex mutation receives same payload
existing tests pass
sonner toast for success

nodes.title: OrderForm → RHF + zod + shadcn Form
nodes.description: Apply the S-06 form pattern to OrderForm (team, sport, quantity, jersey specs, design linking). Closes out the portal surface migration — after this all three portal forms use the canonical form stack and the pilot is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: uses RHF + zodResolver + shadcn Form
zod schema colocated
design multi-select uses appropriate primitive
Convex mutation receives same payload
existing tests pass
sonner toast for success

nodes.title: Marketing Sweep
nodes.description: Refactor HeroSection, CustomizeSection, ProcessSection, FaqSection, QuoteCtaSection, PricingCalculator, and PricingSection to use shadcn primitives. Drop ThemeToggle into MarketingNav. Pricing calculator presentation only — logic untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: every primitive in marketing components sourced from components/ui
ThemeToggle visible in MarketingNav
pricing calculator unit tests still pass
FAQ uses shadcn Accordion or Collapsible
manual smoke in both themes at 375 768 1280

nodes.title: IntakeForm → RHF + zod + shadcn Form
nodes.description: Apply the canonical form pattern to the public intake form. Same Convex mutation, same payload; only form mechanics change. Public-facing and unauthenticated — extra care on a11y and dark-mode error visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: uses RHF + zodResolver + shadcn Form
zod schema colocated
intakes.create mutation receives same payload
success preserved or improved via sonner
existing tests pass
mobile responsive at 375px

nodes.title: JerseyRunPublicForm → RHF + zod + shadcn Form
nodes.description: Convert the public fan submission form at /run/id to the canonical form stack. Public and unauthenticated — preserve closed-state and open-vs-fixed-mode branching exactly as today. Last form to migrate; after this success metric all 5 forms on RHF + zod is achievable.
acceptanceCriteria: uses RHF + zodResolver + shadcn Form
open mode shows free text input fixed mode shows roster Select
blank-name and blank-number warnings preserved
closed-state UI unchanged
existing tests pass

nodes.title: Admin Sweep
nodes.description: Refactor every admin surface — AdminShell, admin order list, admin order detail with 14-stage checklist if 2-12 has landed, admin customers, admin leads, admin jersey runs — to use shadcn primitives. Drop ThemeToggle into the AdminShell header. Largest sweep issue.
acceptanceCriteria: AdminShell uses shadcn Sheet Button Separator
admin tables use consistent shadcn pattern
stage checklist uses shadcn Checkbox with a11y labels
dropdown menus use shadcn DropdownMenu
ThemeToggle in admin header
manual smoke in light and dark

nodes.title: Layout Sweep and Migration Audit
nodes.description: Final pass on components/layout/* plus the migration exit audit. Confirms zero hand-rolled primitives remain anywhere, all five forms on RHF + zod, ThemeToggle works on every surface, and the build is fully green. Closes the migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Logo MarketingFooter MarketingShell MarketingNav SidebarShell use shadcn primitives where applicable
grep audit shows no inline button or input classNames outside components/ui
all 5 forms on RHF + zod
ThemeToggle in marketing nav portal header admin header
dev showcase still renders every primitive
build typecheck lint test pass

nodes.title: Portal Page Dark-Mode Sweep
nodes.description: Swap hardcoded zinc/white classes in app/portal/**/page.tsx for shadcn theme tokens so the portal renders correctly in dark mode. Closes the gap left by S-05 (shell only) — inner page bodies, loading skeletons, StageChip palette, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Every app/portal/**/page.tsx renders correctly in both light and dark mode
Hardcoded zinc/white classes replaced with shadcn tokens (bg-background, bg-card, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, border-border) where appropriate
Brand teal accents preserved
Loading/empty/not-found states all theme-aware
Manual smoke of every portal route in both themes at 375px/768px/1280px
npm run build/typecheck/lint/test pass

nodes.title: Portal Shell Dark-Mode Fix
nodes.description: PortalShell + OrderTimeline + portal-page teal accents missed by S-14: add dark: variants matching AdminShell and migrate hand-rolled brand-teal CTA links to shadcn Button.
acceptanceCriteria: PortalShell dark-mode matches AdminShell pattern
OrderTimeline separators/dots/labels theme-aware
every text-teal-700 link in app/portal/**/page.tsx has dark:text-teal-300 sibling
brand-teal CTA buttons migrated to shadcn Button
build/typecheck/lint/test pass

nodes.title: Jersey Run Submit and Add Another
nodes.description: Public jersey run form (/run/[id]) gains a secondary 'Submit and add another' action. Same mutation + blank-name confirm path as primary submit, then resets per-jersey fields (size, name, number, custom answers) while preserving respondent name and email so a parent/captain can submit several entries in one session. Confirmation surfaces as a sonner toast.
acceptanceCriteria: Secondary 'Submit and add another' button visible next to Submit
Secondary action submits via the same mutation and blank-name confirm dialog
On success the form resets to empty per-jersey fields while keeping respondent name + email
Sonner toast confirms what was just submitted
Primary Submit retains existing single-submit → confirmation behaviour
Both buttons disabled while a submission is in flight
Closed/expired runs still hide both actions
All tests pass; no regressions

nodes.title: Logged-In User Sees Their Jersey Run Responses
nodes.description: Two new Convex queries (listMyResponsesForRun, listMyResponses) plus two UI surfaces: a 'Your responses to this run' panel on /run/[id] for signed-in users with prior responses, and a 'Your jersey run responses' section on the customer portal dashboard. Read-only. Supersedes 3-04.
acceptanceCriteria: listMyResponsesForRun returns current user's responses scoped to run; [] when unauthenticated
listMyResponses returns user's responses across all runs joined with order/run context; [] when unauthenticated
/run/[id] shows 'Your responses to this run' panel when signed-in AND prior responses exist
Panel shows submission date, size, jersey name, jersey number, custom answers (read-only)
Portal dashboard gains 'Your jersey run responses' section across all runs with team/run/jersey context + link to /run/[id]
Empty state when no responses
Anonymous /run/[id] flow unchanged
Email matching normalized consistently with public form's submit path
All tests pass; no regressions

nodes.title: Design Owns Silhouette Specs
nodes.description: Relocate jerseyStyle/neckline/sleeveStyle from the order record onto the design record so a reusable design carries its own cut. Foundational schema swap (no real data) the whole order-page rework depends on.
acceptanceCriteria: designs table gains the three spec fields; orders no longer defines them
designs.create/update validate+persist specs via lib/design/rules; orders.create/update succeed without them
convex dev starts clean; smoke + lib unit tests pass

nodes.title: Design Form Captures Specs
nodes.description: Surface the silhouette specs (now on the design) in the design create/edit form and on the design detail page, using the RHF+zod+shadcn Form pattern from S-07. Files stay optional.
acceptanceCriteria: DesignForm collects jerseyStyle/neckline/sleeve with colocated zod schema
designs.create/update receive the new fields; detail page renders them
files remain optional; existing design tests pass

nodes.title: Order Links Many Designs
nodes.description: Decouple the order from silhouette specs and let one order link zero-to-many designs. estimatedQuantity becomes informational only. Core data change behind 'season kit = one order, many designs'.
acceptanceCriteria: orders.create/update accept 0..many designIds; zero-design order persists
OrderForm drops spec fields and links multiple existing designs
smoke tests cover zero-design and multi-design create paths

nodes.title: New / Edit Order Single Page
nodes.description: Render the order as one scrollable page handling both New and Edit, editable while open, with a progress indicator that blocks the 'design attached' milestone until a design is linked. Locked read-only is layered later by O-06.
acceptanceCriteria: New and Edit render from one page; single scrollable layout, no wizard
all fields editable pre-lock
progress milestone blocked at zero designs, clears once one is linked

nodes.title: Order Detail Design Sections And Handoff
nodes.description: On order detail, present each linked design as its own section under one timeline and give a clear handoff into Run Setup. The jersey run is created lazily on first collect, not on order save.
acceptanceCriteria: order detail renders each linked design as a grouped section
handoff CTA routes to Run Setup
saving an order creates no run; first collect creates exactly one

nodes.title: Freeze Order When Roster Locked
nodes.description: Once the roster is locked, freeze the order: details go read-only and edit mutations are rejected so the production basis cannot drift. Reacts to the external locked run state; standalone designs stay editable. BLOCKED on the external Confirm/Lock slice (jerseyRuns.locked status).
acceptanceCriteria: editable while unlocked; read-only with 'Locked — contact Sidestep' when locked
orders.update rejects edits when the roster is locked
lock freezes roster + order editing only, not the design records

nodes.title: Order Total Derived From Roster
nodes.description: Replace the static estimate with a live total derived from the unified roster: order total and per-design counts are summed from roster rows. BLOCKED on the external Roster Manager slice (unified rosterEntries table).
acceptanceCriteria: order total equals sum of roster row quantities
each design section shows its count from rows tagged with that designId
empty roster reads 0; estimatedQuantity shown only as informational seed

nodes.title: Relabel Remove Design Warning
nodes.description: When a captain relabels or removes a linked design that already has submissions, warn rather than silently orphan: relabel carries submissions to the new label; remove keeps rows and flags them with a removed indicator naming affected submitters. BLOCKED on the external Roster Manager slice (rosterEntries with designId).
acceptanceCriteria: relabel moves existing submissions to the new label with no data loss
remove surfaces a non-destructive warning naming affected submitters
affected rows show a removed indicator; never a silent hard stop

nodes.title: Order Page Visual Pass
nodes.description: Restyle the New/Edit Order page + order detail to match the Claude design mock's visual language (dark dense cards, chips/badges, the X-of-N progress meter, teal accents). The order screen has no mock of its own, so it borrows the Roster Manager surface's design language. Pure presentation, no behaviour change.
acceptanceCriteria: page chrome, chips, and progress meter match the mock's design language
renders correctly in light and dark at 375/768/1280
uses existing shadcn primitives + theme tokens; no functional regressions
